name: Topcolor / top-quark condensate
summary: The Higgs is a t-tbar bound state, with electroweak symmetry breaking driven by a top-quark condensate generated by a new strong gauge sector ('topcolor') that couples preferentially to the third generation. Predicts specific relationships between m_top, m_W, and m_H. Original BHL realisation predicted heavy Higgs (~250 GeV); subsequent topcolor-assisted variants accommodate the observed 125 GeV Higgs by adding a fundamental scalar.

addresses_puzzles.note: Removes the fundamental scalar from the Higgs sector via composite top-quark binding, eliminating the standard quadratic-divergence problem. LHC measurements of Higgs couplings (very SM-like) and the scale of top compositeness (m_t small relative to compositeness scale) make pure topcolor disfavoured; topcolor-assisted variants survive.

touches_benchmarks.note: BHL-type composite Higgs would have non-SM couplings to fermions; LHC pushes parameter space such that the Higgs must be very nearly fundamental, marginalising pure topcolor.
Topcolor predicts non-zero contributions to S and T; Z' bosons constrained by EW precision.
Generation-non-universal Z' couplings generate FCNC at observable levels.

new_symmetries: SU(3)_top x SU(3)_lighter generation gauge symmetry
introduces.note: Topcolor gauge couplings, breaking scales, instanton contributions, Yukawa structure to lighter generations.

history: Bardeen, Hill, Lindner (1990), 'Minimal dynamical symmetry breaking of the standard model', Phys. Rev. D 41, 1647 — original top-condensate proposal. Topcolor-assisted technicolor (Hill 1995) accommodates lighter Higgs. LHC's discovery of a SM-like 125 GeV Higgs in 2012 has substantially constrained the framework.

references.citation: Bardeen, Hill, Lindner, 'Minimal dynamical symmetry breaking of the standard model', Phys. Rev. D 41, 1647 (1990)
Hill, 'Topcolor: top quark condensation in a gauge extension of the standard model', Phys. Lett. B 266, 419 (1991)
